selected_markets.title: Will the **high temp in Austin** be 74-75° on Feb 14, 2026?

valuations.rationale: Mid-probability of 1% seems artificially low for a narrow 1-degree temperature band in Austin during mid-February. Historical data shows Austin's average high in mid-February is ~60°F with typical range 50-70°F, making 74-75°F unusually warm but not impossible (would require ~14°F above normal). The positive enrich_bias (0.0408) suggests market underpricing. Fair estimate ~3.5% accounts for low but non-negligible probability of anomalous warmth. Low confidence due to limited volume (601) and uncertainty in market microstructure.
